name: Microsoft minimal (E3 only)
description: Microsoft 365 E3 without Defender for Endpoint P2 or Purview add-ons. Detection coverage is limited; relies on network-level signals and policy/comms-driven controls.
compensating_controls: Network-egress filtering at the firewall / proxy using the ConsumerLLMDomains watchlist (DET-NET-001 + DET-NET-002).
Conditional Access policies blocking unmanaged-device access to corporate apps (DET-MS-CA-001) to reduce BYO bypass.
Defender for Cloud Apps basic shadow-app discovery via manual log upload (DET-MS-MCAS-MANUAL-001).
Heavy reliance on AUP enforcement via comms and manager talking points.
Strongly recommended: upgrade path to E5 Security or Defender for Endpoint P2 + Purview add-ons before a mature program is achievable.

things_to_know.title: Endpoint DLP not available
things_to_know.content: Without Purview DLP, you cannot block sensitive-data uploads at the endpoint. Compensate with network-layer filtering and explicit AUP.

things_to_know.title: Limited investigative depth
things_to_know.content: Without DeviceNetworkEvents from Defender for Endpoint, you cannot pivot from a single user's alert to all their devices' AI-related connections. Investigations rely on firewall logs.

things_to_know.title: AUP-driven control
things_to_know.content: On this stack, the AI Acceptable Use Policy and approved-tool comms are the primary control. Technical detection is supporting evidence, not the gate.

policy_token_replacements.dlp_tool: network egress filtering at your secure web gateway
policy_token_replacements.casb_tool: Defender for Cloud Apps (manual log upload)
